items.headline: FF7 Rebirth Demo Drops on Switch 2 and Xbox — Play First
items.subhead: Square Enix is letting you touch the Midgar magic before June 3, and the timing says everything about where gaming is headed.
items.tldr: The Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth demo is now live on Nintendo Switch 2 and Xbox ahead of its June 3 launch, and the move signals a major shift in platform accessibility for one of gaming's most important franchises. Switch 2's ability to run Rebirth meaningfully changes the conversation about Nintendo's new hardware. This isn't just a port — it's a statement about where AAA gaming is heading.

items.headline: Drake and Future Are Back. The Question Is Why.
items.subhead: A sequel to What A Time To Be Alive sounds like legacy move — but the timing tells a different story.
items.tldr: Drake and Future are teasing a sequel to their 2015 collaborative mixtape What A Time To Be Alive, with Metro Boomin reportedly in the mix. S&B breaks down why the beat economics, the post-beef climate, and the danger of sequels make this project fascinating and risky in equal measure.

items.headline: Travis Scott Just Claimed the Soccer Pitch Too
items.subhead: The Phantom 6 Low Elite collab isn't a sneaker drop — it's a territory grab.
items.tldr: Travis Scott and Nike are crossing into football territory with the Phantom 6 Low Elite, dropping Summer 2026 at $200 in a Multi-Color/Metallic Gold colorway. The collab is less about cleats and more about where sneaker culture is heading next — and Travis, as usual, got there first.

items.headline: Anime Took Over Streetwear And Nobody Asked For Permission
items.subhead: BAPE x One Piece. JJK x Nike. The crossover is no longer a side project — it's the business model for the next decade.
items.tldr: Anime took over streetwear because the math finally clicked — global IP, no licensing wars, and a Black-nerd consumer demo brands ignored for too long. The window stays cheap for 18 months.
